Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Expand Scope Of Guardian Ad Litem
Off Calendar Feb-18-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Mayo appeared by zoom for Leland Valentine (original petitioner).
Avid Alavi (Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL") for Minor Beneficiary David Valentine, Jr.) appeared by zoom.

The Court noted that Mr. Rothstein and Mr. Kim are not present and stated that if they had been present, it would have asked if the other petitions could go off calendar. Ms. Alavi stated that she assumes they can go off calendar, but they are not her petitions and that she sent an e-mail to both Mr. Rothstein and Mr. Kim. Mr. Mayo stated that she could withdraw her petition. Ms. Alavi stated that she reached out to Counsel about her staying on as the GAL, that she does not know the value of the subtrust, that there are two parcels of property that need to be sold and that the value of the subtrust could be up to $1,000,000.00. Ms. Mayo stated that the properties are in really bad shape, that there are some tax liabilities, that there are escape taxes that were overlooked and that there is about #300,000.00 in cash. Ms. Mayo also stated that because the Minor who is now twelve and living in the Philippines with the Mother and the Stepfather, Ms. Alavi has been working diligently in getting a passport and a social security number for the Minor to get the money put in a protected account. Ms. Alavi stated that the subtrust is not going to be under Court supervision, that the Valentine trust is not under Court supervision, that she has selected Minna Fernan and that Ms. Fernan is going to be managing the subtrust. Ms. Alavi also stated that Ms. Fernan is going to be doing annual accountings to a Minor and that is why she should stay on, that she is planning on filing fee petition to present to the Court, that her fees will be paid out of the Valentine trust and that once the Valentine trust is distributed, she anticipates that the fess will be much lower. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that the Valentine trust instrument sets out the terms of the subtrust, referred to section 9.6 A-22 of the Valentine trust, stated that there are three options for maintaining the Minor's funds (a guardianship, a CUTMA account, subtrust) and that section 9.6 E of the Valentine trust allows them to create a separate trust. Furthermore, Ms. Alavi spoke of the terms of the subtrust that are set out by the trust instrument, stated that bond is already required, and Ms. Fernan will be bonded, and that she has no problem with the subtrust being under Court supervision, but it will be an additional expense to the Minor. Ms. Alavi also stated that she is hoping to get social security benefits for the Mother, that the Mother can use those benefits for the support of the Minor, that the Mother will be asking for less and less, and that her plan is to keep the Minor's funds as whole as possible. The Petition to Remove Trustee for Breach of Trust, etc., the Amended Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Administrative Trust), etc., the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Trust B), etc., the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Survivor's Trust), etc., and the Petition for First and Final Account of Trustee Patricia Valentine, etc. are continued to May 12, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on creation of and funding of the subtrust for David Valentine, Jr. is also set for May 12, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement and Expand Scope of Guardian Ad Litem is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition 1. To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And Appoint A Successor Trustee Or Professional Fiduciary;
Off Calendar Feb-19-2020 Continued To May-13-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Kim for Sam Valentine (Trustee).
Mary Mayo for Leland Valentine (Beneficiary/Administrator of the Patricia Valentine Estate).
Avid Alavi Guardian Ad Litem for David Valentine, Jr.

The Parties indicated that they have agreed to two Mediators (Ed Corey and Judge Whitley) and that they are hoping to start mediation in the next month or so. Mr. Kim stated that Mr. Corey is an Attorney in his office, but the Parties have agreed to waive any conflicts. Ms. Alavi stated that they have made progress with regard to the accounting, that they have now retained an Immigration Attorney, and are getting a passport and a social security number for the Minor, and she is hoping to open a CUTMA account. The Court questions why not a blocked account. Ms. Alavi stated that it would need to be a guardianship, spoke of the expense of getting a blocked account in the Philippines and stated that there is the issue of who would be the Guardian. Ms. Mayo stated that the words "the alleged titled owner" were used because they haven't pulled the deed. The Court informed the Parties that it would not be inclined to drag this out so that the property could be subdivided, or condo converted. Ms. Alavi stated that they have talked about the option for Sam to get financing if he wanted to keep a property. The Court noted that Leland's interest could differ from Patricia's estate interest. Ms. Mayo stated that Patricia's accounting addresses this and sets forth a surcharge, that the penalties and late fees for the taxes would be deducted from the Survivor's Trust and that 1/3 goes to David, Jr., 1/3 goes to Sam and 1/3 goes to Leland. Ms. Alavi stated that the biggest issue is to come up with a number for allocated rents. Mr. Kim raised the issue of his Client having the ability to file Patricia's accounting and Leland wearing two hats. The Court informed the Parties that one hearing will be set in the Trial Department, that the Trial Department will hear all of the petitions with factual disputes, that they will not get piece meal resolutions to legal issues and that factual issues will be tried by one trier of facts. Ms. Mayo stated that the Parties have agreed on a Realtor, that the Realtor will come out to the property and recommend repairs, that Mr. Kim will speak with his Client to see how this is going to work, that she feels like the ball is moving forward and that she feels more confident. Ms. Mayo also stated that the survivor spouse trust has all of the property, that all surcharges will go against the survivor spouse trust, that there was a $250,000.00 preliminary distribution to Leland from Trust-B from the sale of the Lodi property and that the same needs to be made to David, and raised the issue of the bank accounts having been wiped out and the issue Trust-B making a distribution of $250,000.00 to Patricia's estate. The Court noted that there is no petition and that the trusts are not under Court supervision. The Petition to Remove Trustee for Breach of Trust, etc., the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Administrative Trust), etc., the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Trust B), etc., the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Surviving Spouse's Trust), etc. and the Petition for First and Final Account of Trustee Patricia Valentine, etc. are continued to May 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint status report identifying the issues that need to be tried, what the claims are from each petition, the disputes of fact, witnesses, etc. is to be filed by May 6, 2020. Ms. Alavi stated that the issues are not that complicated and that she feels confident that they will be able to get things resolved.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Singh. Not reported.

Petition 1. To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And Appoint A Successor Trustee Or Professional Fiduciary;
Off Calendar Dec-16-2019 Continued To Feb-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Kim appeared by telephone for Samuel Valentine (Trustee).
Mary Mayo for Leland Valentine (Beneficiary) and as the Administrator of Patricia's estate.
Avid Alavi (Guardian Ad Litem) for David Valentine, Jr. (Minor).

The Court advised the Parties as to its policy and its meaning of providing a joint status report. Ms. Mayo stated that she was not able to participate with the Weintraub firm and that she was not allowed to put what she wanted in the report. Ms. Alavi stated that she was trying to get more information so that she could get firm numbers to come to resolution, and that she is trying to get numbers on the late payments for the property taxes to determine any surcharge amounts. Ms. Mayo stated that she has been assisting with the late payment issue and that the bigger problem is the preliminary distribution issue. Mr. Kim stated that they have had a standing offer to mediate, that if they can't get it resolved there is some discovery to be propounded and a need to set an evidentiary hearing, and that there are so many factual issues. Ms. Mayo stated that she does mediation often and asks that written offers be provided to have some frame work, but she was told there is no privilege under section 1152 and that they would not provide bank statements. The Court warns Mr. Kim not to interrupt others or he will not be allowed to make telephonic appearances and noted that discovery should've been ongoing. Ms. Mayo stated that there are three trusts, that there is a 2017 pot of money that needs to be distributed, that the Edmond Trust is not closed and that the Administrative Trust needs to be terminated. The Court reminds the Parties that the intent of the Settlors is that the Court not get involved. Ms. Mayo stated that she needs information on the equivalency of the use of the apartments on a schedule G and that the accounting includes rental receipts outside of the accounting period. The Petition to Remove Trustee for Breach of Trust, etc., the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Administrative Trust), etc., the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Trust B), etc., and the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Surviving Spouse's Trust), etc. are continued to February 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint status report is to be provided by January 27, 2020. All Parties are to cooperate in providing the joint status report and are to address all of the petitions that are pending and provide a plan to get each of them to resolution. The status report is also to address what the claims are, what the disputes of facts are, who the witnesses are and time estimates of testimony in entirety, the total time of trial, etc. The Court informed the parties that it will not set the trial out any further than April of 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Patricia Valentine'S Accounting
Off Calendar Oct-21-2019 Continued To Dec-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Kim appeared by telephone for Samuel Valentine (Petitioner).
Mary Mayo for Leland Valentine (Petitioner/Objector).
Avid Alavi for David Valentine, Jr. (Minor Beneficiary).

Ms. Mayo stated that Patricia never objected to these petitions, that Patricia's estate has competing petitions which are to be heard on Wednesday and that her estate will get a 25% distribution. Ms. Mayo also stated that the current Trustee has all of the statements to do an accounting and that there is a proposed schedule that sets forth the bulk of the accounting. Mr. Kim stated that he received information on Friday that Karen will be withdrawing her petition for probate in Karen's estate, that he believes Samuel will be filing a petition for probate, that Patricia's estate has an equal share in trust distribution and that they need someone in Patricia's shoes to authorize resolution. Ms. Alavi stated that the matter is far from resolution, that the trust is not being administered properly, that there are penalties for taxes/late payment of the property taxes, that there are no efforts to market the property, that there are no for sale signs and that Samuel and his Daughter continue to live rent free. Ms. Mayo stated that for the last six months third party rents are not being put into the trust account. The Court noted that administration of this trust is a mess and has been for some time, that the issue of removal is for breach of duties and it needs a proposal, and asked how to resolve the issue of who is responsible for this trust. Mr. Kim stated that he has asked if the parties were interested in mediation two times, that there was no response, that trial is not the best option and that he feels mediation is the best option. Ms. Mayo stated that because of clear malfeasance she cannot in good conscience and moral standing participate in mediation, that they are ready to move forward with a Professional Fiduciary, that accountings are not forthcoming and that there is a huge problem. Ms. Alavi stated that they need some definite numbers for possible surcharge against Samuel and Patricia, and questions how they can have meaningful mediation without definite numbers. The Court stated that they need to set a time frame for discovery and set the matter out for trial. Status on filing of Patricia Valentine's accounting, the Petition to Remove Trustee for Breach of Trust, etc., the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Administrative Trust), etc., the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Trust B), etc., and the Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Successor Trustee (Surviving Spouse's Trust), etc. are continued to December 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to meet and confer and submit a trial plan that indicates what discovery will look like, a time frame for discovery and a time estimate for trial. A status report is to be filed by December 2, 2019. The Court stated that the accountings need to get in shape.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Patricia Valentine'S Accounting
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Continued To Oct-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Gary Rothstein for and with Samuel Valentine. Avid Alavi GAL for David Valentine Jr.. Mary Mayo for Leland Valentine. Gilbert Jay for the Estate of Patricia Valentine.

Mr. Jay stated that there is a petition filed by Leland Valentine before this Court, that he filed a counter petition based on the holographic will, and that he will be representing Karen Valentine (a solo beneficiary and niece of Patricia Valentine). The Court stated that it could not go forward until Ms. Valentine's Estate has representation. Ms. Mayo stated that this would pose some problems, that there are some issues to resolve such as the accounting filed by Samuel Valentine (the current Trustee), that Samuel Valentine is Karen Valentine's Father, that the accounting was filed before Patricia Valentine's death, and that Patrcia Valentine did not file an objection. The Court noted that someone could file objections to the accounting. Ms. Mayo stated that the current Trustee could make distributions. Mr. Rothstein proposed mediation, stated that that Samuel Valentine was reluctant to do so before, that they could make a preliminary distribution to the Minor David Valentine Jr. and Leland Valentine, that they don't know what the distribution shares are, and that the preliminary distribution would be in good faith to head toward mediation. Ms. Mayo noted that there was $1,600,000.00 in proceeds from the sale of the Lodi vacant lot, that the distribution is to be made this week, that it is in her petition, and that there is damage in withholding the distribution to the Minor beneficiary. Ms. Mayo addressed there being an evidentiary hearing on the accountings. The Court commented that this is not going to go anywhere without mediation and that it is a mess. Ms. Mayo addressed the will contest, stated that there are two petitions for probate before the court, and that September 11, 2019 will not see letters signed. The Court stated that it will not go forward without Patricia Valentine's Estate represented here and that it encourages the parties to talk. Ms. Alavi agreed with the Court stating that someone should be here for Patricia Valentine, that the big issue is with regard to Samuel Valentine, that Samuel Valentine has conflicting roles, that he has allowed family members including himself to live rent free in the property at the detriment of the Trust beneficiaries, that he claims that the settlor agreed to this, and that a professional fiduciary should be in place to clean up the accountings & move this along. Ms. Mayo stated that she supports Ms. Alavi's conflict regarding Sam Valentine's interests, that he is withholding cash & not distributing it to the beneficiaries, and that $250,000.00 should be distributed to the Minor beneficiary. Mr. Rothstein stated that the family always wanted to take care of the Minor. The Court stated that some had more access than others in this matter, that it is all for making it more even, that it does appreciate how complicated that it is, and that it wants everyone to get their right to be heard. Ms. Mayo addressed the amended accounting. Mr. Rothstein stated that they filed a supplement. The Court addressed Mr. Rothstein stating that an Amended Accounting was to be filed. Mr. Rothstein stated that he could have it on file this week. The Court stated that amended accounting was to be on file by August 21, 2019 and notice of the amended accounting as well. Mr. Rothstein addressed the Court and requested that matter be referred to retired Alameda County Judge Whitley as the mediator. The Court stated that it cannot make that referral to retired Judge Whitley and then continued all petitions before the Court today to October 7, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Mr. Rothstein stated that Patricia Valentine has taken a turn for the worst, that they are not sure if she will come out of her current medical condition, that his Client will try to get some bank statements for the accounts that Patricia held and that his Client is going to try to recover the unclaimed property. Mr. Rothstein also stated that Mr. Valentine will provide monthly account statements to the Beneficiaries, that Mr. Valentine has changed the names on the Chase account to his name only, that the Wife's name is no longer on the account, that the account will be changed to Mr. Valentine's name as Trustee, and that he believes the Mother of David (Minor) can be the Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL") and that there is no conflict because the Mother is not an heir. Mr. Rothstein stated that Mr. Valentine provided a report on Friday, that he was able to obtain a $300,000.00 loan on one of the properties, that the Trustee will probably be selling one of the properties and that courtesy copies were submitted to the Court on Friday. The Court stated that there is no page 10 of the objections, that it needs proposed orders on the accountings and that it should be a GAL in San Francisco.
